info.name: EasyPlug API - Complete Collection
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for EasyPlug API with automatic token and ID management.

## Socket.IO Real-time Testing
This collection includes a dedicated **🔌 Socket.IO - Real-time** folder.
To test Socket.IO events in Postman:
1. Open Postman Desktop App (v10+)
2. Click **New → Socket.IO** to create a Socket.IO request
3. Use server URL: `http://localhost:8000`
4. Under **Connection > Auth**, add key `token` with value `{{authToken}}`
5. Add listeners for `new_message` and `new_notification`
6. Emit `join_chat` with the chatId to subscribe to chat events

See the **🔌 Socket.IO - Real-time** folder for full event documentation.

item.name: 🔔 Notifications
item.description:
User notifications system.

**Socket.IO Integration:**
Every new notification is also pushed in real-time via Socket.IO:
- Event: `new_notification`
- Room: `user:{userId}` (joined automatically on connection)
- Payload: `{ notification: { notificationId, userId, type, title, message, actionUrl, metadata, isRead, createdAt } }`

Notification types: `message`, `transaction`, `listing`, `account`, `system`

request.description: Get all notifications for user (20 per page). Notifications are also delivered in real-time via Socket.IO `new_notification` event on the `user:{userId}` room.
Get only unread notifications
Get count of unread notifications (for badge display). The count increases in real-time when new `new_notification` Socket.IO events are received.
Mark a single notification as read. Sets `isRead: true` and `readAt` timestamp.

exec: pm.test('All notifications marked as read', function () {

request.description: Mark all notifications as read
Delete a notification

item.name: 💬 Chats & Messages
item.description:
Chat system - one chat per listing/buyer/seller combination.

**Socket.IO Integration:**
- When `POST /chat-messages` is called, the server emits:
  1. `new_message` → to `chat:{chatId}` room (all participants)
  2. `new_notification` → to `user:{receiverId}` room (receiver only)
- Clients must join the chat room first by emitting `join_chat` with the chatId
- See the **🔌 Socket.IO** folder for event details and connection setup

exec: console.log('ℹ️  Join this chat room via Socket.IO: emit join_chat with chatId:', jsonData.chat.chatId);

request.description: Create a new chat or get existing chat for this listing/seller combination. After getting the chatId, emit `join_chat` via Socket.IO to receive real-time messages.

exec: console.log('📋 Last message preview:', firstChat.lastMessage ? firstChat.lastMessage.message : 'No messages');

request.description: Get all chats for authenticated user (as buyer or seller). Each chat includes `lastMessage` and `unreadCount`.
Get chat details with all messages. Messages are ordered by `createdAt ASC`.

exec: console.log('   → new_message emitted to room chat:' + pm.collectionVariables.get('chatId'));
console.log('   → new_notification emitted to room user:' + pm.collectionVariables.get('sellerId'));

request.description:
Send a message in a chat.

**Triggers two Socket.IO events:**
1. `new_message` → emitted to `chat:{chatId}` room
   - Payload: `{ message: { messageId, chatId, senderId, receiverId, message, isRead, createdAt } }`
2. `new_notification` → emitted to `user:{receiverId}` room
   - Payload: `{ notification: { notificationId, type: 'message', title, message, actionUrl, metadata } }`

**Required fields:** `chatId`, `message`
**Optional:** `receiverId` (needed for notification)

exec: console.log('✅ Latest message ID saved:', lastMsg.messageId);

query.description: Number of messages to skip for pagination

request.description:
Retrieve paginated messages for a chat, ordered by `createdAt ASC`.

**Query params:**
- `limit` - messages per page (default: 100)
- `offset` - skip N messages (default: 0)

exec: console.log('✅ Messages marked as read:', jsonData.updatedCount);

request.description:
Mark all unread messages in a chat as read for the authenticated user.

- Only marks messages sent **by others** (not the current user's own messages)
- Returns `updatedCount` with how many messages were updated
- User must be a participant (buyer or seller) in the chat

Mark a specific message as read.

- Returns `403` if you try to mark your own sent message as read
- Returns `403` if the message was not addressed to you

item.name: 🔌 Socket.IO - Real-time
item.description:
# Socket.IO Real-time Testing Guide

The EasyPlug server runs Socket.IO v4 on `http://localhost:8000` (no /api/v1 prefix).

## Connection Setup in Postman

1. Open **Postman Desktop App v10+**
2. Click **New** → **Socket.IO**
3. Enter URL: `http://localhost:8000`
4. Under the **Connection** tab → **Handshake** → **Auth**:
   - Add key: `token`, value: `<paste your JWT here>` (from `authToken` variable)
5. Click **Connect**

On connection, the server automatically joins you to room `user:{yourUserId}` for notifications.

## Events Reference

### Client → Server (Emit)
| Event | Data | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `join_chat` | `chatId` (number/string) | Join a chat room to receive messages |
| `leave_chat` | `chatId` (number/string) | Leave a chat room |

### Server → Client (Listen)
| Event | Payload | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `new_message` | `{ message: {...} }` | New chat message in a joined room |
| `new_notification` | `{ notification: {...} }` | New notification for current user |

## Test Workflow

1. **Login** via REST → get `authToken`
2. **Connect** Socket.IO with `token: <authToken>` in handshake auth
3. **Listen** for `new_notification` and `new_message`
4. **Create a chat** via REST → note `chatId`
5. **Emit** `join_chat` with the `chatId`
6. **Send a message** via REST (`POST /chat-messages`)
7. **Verify** `new_message` fires in the chat room
8. **Verify** `new_notification` fires on the receiver's user room

See child requests in this folder for per-event test scripts and REST-side verification.

exec: console.log('Auth token set:', token ? 'YES ✅' : 'NO ❌ (run Login first)');
console.log('  new_notification  → triggered by POST /chat-messages (receiver side)');

request.description:
This request is a **setup verification** step, not a Socket.IO request itself.

Run this to confirm your `authToken` is valid before opening a Socket.IO connection in Postman.

## How to open a Socket.IO connection in Postman:

1. Click **New** → **Socket.IO** in the Postman sidebar
2. Server URL: `{{socketUrl}}` → `http://localhost:8000`
3. **Handshake > Auth** tab: add `token = <your JWT token>`
4. Click **Connect**

## Expected connection result:
- Status: `connected`
- Server auto-joins you to room `user:{yourUserId}`
- You will start receiving `new_notification` events

exec: console.log('  new_message events whenever POST /chat-messages is called');
// Verify the chat exists and user is a participant
pm.test('Chat exists and user is a participant (safe to join room)', function () {
console.log('✅ Chat verified. Now emit join_chat with chatId:', chatId);

request.description:
**REST Verification Step** - Confirms the chat exists before emitting `join_chat`.

## Socket.IO: Emit `join_chat`

After verifying the chat via this REST call, go to your Socket.IO connection in Postman and:

1. In the **Messages** tab, select the **Event name** field
2. Enter event name: `join_chat`
3. Enter message (the chatId): `{{chatId}}`
4. Click **Send**

## Server-side logic:
```javascript
socket.on('join_chat', (chatId) => {
  if (chatId) socket.join(`chat:${chatId}`);
});
```

After joining, any `POST /chat-messages` to this chatId will broadcast a `new_message` event to this room.

exec: console.log('After leaving, you will NO LONGER receive:');
console.log('  new_message events for chatId:', chatId || '<chatId>');
console.log('  (but you still receive new_notification on your user room)');
console.log('  1. Socket.IO connected with valid JWT');
console.log('  2. Emitted join_chat with chatId:', chatId || '<chatId>');
console.log('This REST request sends a message which triggers:');
pm.test('Message sent - new_message Socket.IO event triggered', function () {
console.log('🔌 Socket.IO events fired on server:');
console.log('Check your Socket.IO listener for new_message event!');
console.log('Full new_message payload:', JSON.stringify({ message: jsonData.message }, null, 2));

request.description:
**Trigger the `new_message` Socket.IO event** by sending a REST message.

## Setup before running:
1. Open a Socket.IO connection in Postman (see [GUIDE] request)
2. Add listener for `new_message`
3. Emit `join_chat` with `{{chatId}}`
4. Run this request

## Expected Socket.IO `new_message` payload:
```json
{
  "message": {
    "messageId": 42,
    "chatId": 1,
    "senderId": "<your-user-id>",
    "receiverId": "<seller-user-id>",
    "message": "Hello!",
    "isRead": false,
    "createdAt": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

## Room targeted:
`chat:{chatId}` — all clients who emitted `join_chat` with this chatId receive the event.

exec: console.log('The receiver must be connected to Socket.IO to get this event.');
message: '<sender> sent you a message about "<listing title>"',

pm.test('Latest notification is a message notification', function () {
console.log('✅ Latest notification ID saved:', latest.notificationId);
console.log('This notification was ALSO sent via Socket.IO:');

request.description:
**Verify `new_notification` was created** after sending a message.

Run this as the **receiver's user** (log in as the seller if the buyer sent the message) to confirm the notification exists in the DB — which also means the `new_notification` Socket.IO event was emitted.

## Socket.IO `new_notification` setup:
1. Connect to Socket.IO with your JWT (no need to join any room — the server auto-joins `user:{yourUserId}`)
2. Add listener for `new_notification`
3. The event fires automatically whenever someone sends you a message or another system event occurs

## Expected `new_notification` payload:
```json
{
  "notification": {
    "notificationId": "<uuid>",
    "userId": "<receiver-id>",
    "type": "message",
    "title": "New Message",
    "message": "John Doe sent you a message about 'iPhone 15 Pro Max'",
    "actionUrl": "/chats/1",
    "metadata": {
      "chatId": 1,
      "senderId": "<sender-id>",
      "senderName": "John Doe",
      "listingId": "<listing-id>",
      "listingTitle": "iPhone 15 Pro Max",
      "messagePreview": "Hi, is this item still available?"
    },
    "isRead": false,
    "createdAt": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

## Room targeted:
`user:{receiverId}` — only the receiver gets this event.

request.description:
**Final step in the full real-time chat test flow.**

Run this after completing the Socket.IO setup and sending a message. It verifies that:
1. The unread notification count has increased (confirming REST + Socket.IO DB write worked)
2. All collection variables are properly set

## Complete Test Execution Order:
1. 🔐 Authentication → **Login** (sets `authToken`, `userId`)
2. 📦 Listings → **Create Listing** (sets `listingId`)
3. 💬 Chats → **Create or Get Chat** (sets `chatId`)
4. 🔌 Socket.IO → **[GUIDE] Socket.IO Connection Setup** (read instructions, connect manually)
5. 🔌 Socket.IO → **[EVENT] join_chat** (read instructions, emit manually)
6. 🔌 Socket.IO → **[EVENT] new_message - Trigger & Verify** (sends REST message, triggers socket events)
7. 🔌 Socket.IO → **[EVENT] new_notification - Trigger & Verify** (confirms notification in DB)
8. 🔌 Socket.IO → **[FLOW] Full Real-time Chat Test** ← **You are here** (final assertion)

Get all ratings in the system

Get all users (admin)
Get user details by ID
Update authenticated user's profile
Create a new address for user
Get all addresses for authenticated user
Get all available subscription plans
Create a new subscription plan (admin)
